text.content:
Beat 25 — The Three-Crisis Siege

Act: Act 3 | Type: Montage Scene

Overview

Beat 25 — The Three-Crisis Siege of Hrafnborg

An Extreme (Custom) three-track simultaneous montage. The party must split their attention across three crises erupting at once. This is an impossible triage — with 4 heroes over 2 rounds (8 tests total), fully holding all three crises requires ~9 successes, which is mathematically impossible. At least one crisis will be neglected.

Special Rules

  • Each hero tests once per round (standard montage rules). No repeating skills.
  • Before each round, each hero declares which crisis they are addressing. Their test applies to that crisis's track.
  • Track successes and failures per crisis, not as a single total.
  • Each crisis needs 3 successes to be successfully held.
  • The crisis the party neglects MOST determines how Grafvitnir gets through in Beat 26.

Crisis Scoring (at end of Round 2)

SuccessesResult
0–1Major compromise — this access point is wide open for Grafvitnir
2Minor compromise — partially held, limited benefit to Grafvitnir
3+Successfully held — this position remains secure

Beat 23 Bonus

If the party achieved a success in Brynja's negotiation (Beat 23), the refugees provide logistical support granting +1 to one test in Crisis A or Crisis C.

Oath of Witness Bonus

If the party swore the Oath of Witness to Rickety Frets (Beat 12), a hero who invokes the oath when rallying defenders gains an second chance (two rolls, take best) on the Crisis A "Rally the exhausted gate defenders" Presence/Inspire test.

Running This Scene

This is a narrative montage — a series of challenges the party faces as a group. Rather than a single roll, play this out as a sequence of scenes where each character contributes.

GM Guidance

  • Go around the table and ask each player how their character is helping.
  • Call for ability checks as appropriate — suggested modified [Resolute←15] (hard).
  • Let creative approaches succeed with good roleplaying, even without a roll.
  • Use the complications below as prompts — not every one needs to come up.
  • The goal is collaborative storytelling, not a pass/fail test.

Relevant Skills:

  • Strong
  • Quick or Discreet
  • Cunning or Cunning
  • Vigilant, Vigilant, or Vigilant
  • Persuasive, Persuasive, or Persuasive

Complications & Challenges

Use these as dramatic beats during the montage. Present them as situations the party must navigate together.

Round 1: The Crisis Escalates

Three crises erupt simultaneously. Each hero declares which crisis they address before testing.

Crisis A: East Gate — The Draugr Tide

Hundreds of organized undead pressing in synchronized waves. The gate brace is failing. Defenders are rotating too fast, exhaustion and cold setting in.

ChallengeCharacteristicDescription
Physically hold the gate brace during wave impactMight / LeadOne of the timed impacts is coming. A hero stands in the brace point and uses their body to reinforce it.
Move through the wall positions to thin the columnAgility / DiscreetThe undead are lining up for the next wave. A hero moves along the ramparts and attacks from above, thinning the column.
Rally the exhausted gate defendersPresence / InspireThe defenders are at breaking point. A speech, a gesture, a reminder of what they defend — can restore their will to hold. (Oath of Witness second chance (two rolls, take best) applies here.)
Identify the draugr wave patternReason / TacticsThe waves are timed. Understanding the rhythm helps anticipate and brace — when the next impact comes, how long defenders have to recover.

Crisis B: West Wall — Rot Grubs in the Foundation

In the basement of the west tower, Rot Grubs are eating the foundation stone from inside. Hildvarð's Heitfolk are shoring up what they can, but they need help.

ChallengeCharacteristicDescription
Identify the grub colony's tunneling patternReason / CunningWyrd-sense and observation: where are the grubs tunneling next? What sections will fail first?
Shore up failing sections with timber and rubble bracingMight / LiftHildvarð and the Heitfolk are doing this, but they need help. A hero reinforces key support points.
Follow the Rot-signature to its origin pointIntuition / Wyrd-senseTrace the Rot corruption backward to where it's strongest. Disrupting the colony's Wyrd-connection weakens them.
Carefully collapse tunnels without bringing down the wallAgility / PrecisionTunnel collapse is a controlled demolition problem. Do it wrong and the whole tower comes down.

Crisis C: Main Gate — The Wight-Commander's Engine

A Wight-Commander has assembled a bone-and-timber battering ram. Twelve draugr operate it in synchronized movement. It has hit twice. The gate is showing cracks.

ChallengeCharacteristicDescription
Ranged shots from the towers to disrupt ram carriersAgility / PrecisionThe twelve draugr are coordinated but exposed. Precision shots from the towers can disrupt the formation.
Drop heavy debris from the wall onto the ramMight / StrongTimber, stone blocks, barrels — drop them to damage the ram structure.
Identify the Commander as the coordination centerReason / TacticsThe ram only works because the Commander is directing movement. If killed or driven back, the ram loses efficiency.
Attempt to intimidate the CommanderPresence / IntimidateHard difficulty — it is intelligent and motivated. Attempting to make it hesitate.

Escalation

If the scene needs more tension, introduce these additional complications:

Round 2: The Breaking Point

Each crisis has escalated. The undead are pushing harder. The stone is cracking deeper. The ram is swinging faster. Each hero again declares which crisis they address.

Crisis A: East Gate (Round 2)

ChallengeCharacteristicDescription
Hold the line — morale breaks or holdsPresenceThe moment where morale breaks or holds. The defenders need a hero standing with them.
Brace the failing gate with raw strengthMightThe brace is failing. A hero might hold it through one more impact.
Scatter the column and break formationAgilityA runner-and-climber hero can scatter the column, break the formation, disrupt the waves.

Crisis B: West Wall (Round 2)

ChallengeCharacteristicDescription
Predict the next failure pointReason / CunningThe grubs are reaching a critical point. Understanding what's happening, predicting the next failure.
More bracing — force and will against the inevitableMightMore shoring. Pushing against the collapse with force and determination.
Read the Wyrd-signature and find the weak pointIntuitionSensing the Rot's movement, reading the Wyrd-signature, finding a weak point in the colony to target.

Crisis C: Main Gate (Round 2)

ChallengeCharacteristicDescription
Confront the Wight-Commander directlyPresence / IntimidateA direct confrontation. Can the hero make it hesitate? Doubt? Fear?
Sabotage the ram from belowAgilityCut the lashing, break the structure from underneath.
Drop the heaviest thing you can throw onto the ramMightFull-strength impact from above onto the ram mechanism.

Outcomes

Based on how the party performed, use the following as a guide:

Round 1 Interlude: After the First Assault

Describe the state of all three crises based on Round 1 successes:

  • East gate: Either holding or beginning to buckle. Either the defenders are rallied or they're beginning to break.
  • Main gate: Either the ram is damaged or it's hitting harder. Either the Commander is distracted or it's commanding perfect synchronization.
  • Great hall: The sound from below hasn't stopped. It's gotten louder. The cracks in the floor are spreading. You can see the pattern now — it's growing, spreading from the center outward, in shapes that look almost like roots.

Söldís is still there. She hasn't moved. She's watching the cracks spread. Harald is calculating: can the walls hold another hour? Two?

Final Outcomes by Crisis

Crisis A: East Gate

  • 0–1 successes: East gate buckles. Draugr pour into the outer ward. In Beat 26, the party must fight through the outer ward to reach the great hall.
  • 2 successes: Gate holds but barely. Minor draugr incursion in outer ward, manageable.
  • 3+ successes: Gate holds cleanly. No draugr in the city.

Crisis B: West Wall

  • 0–1 successes: Tunnels partially widened. Grafvitnir's emergence in Beat 26 is fast and violent. No warning.
  • 2 successes: Tunnels sealed at surface, remain open below. Grafvitnir emerges after one extra round of warning.
  • 3+ successes: Tunnels sealed and narrowed. Party has clear warning before Grafvitnir rises. Time to position.

Note: Even with 3+ successes, the deep tunnels remain. Grafvitnir follows these passages upward regardless. The question is how much warning the party gets, not whether he comes.

Crisis C: Main Gate

  • 0–1 successes: Main gate cracks. Partial breach. Organized draugr enter the inner ward in Beat 26.
  • 2 successes: Ram disrupted once. Gate damaged but holds.
  • 3+ successes: Ram destroyed or Commander driven back. Main gate holds completely.

Closing Transition: After Round 2, the sound from below the great hall stops. The silence is worse than the sound. The floor begins to crack. The Ever-Ember burns. Söldís is still there, clay pot in hands. The cracks spread in patterns that look like roots. Beat 26 begins.
